text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Fresh eggs, even those with clean, uncracked shells, may contain Salmonella.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Fresh eggs, even those with clean, uncracked shells, may contain bacteria called Salmonella that can cause foodborne illness, often called “food poisoning.”","entailment":"The quote explicitly says that fresh eggs with clean, uncracked shells may contain the bacteria Salmonella.","population":"fresh eggs","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"presence of Salmonella","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"may","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Most people infected with Salmonella develop diarrhea, fever, abdominal cramps, and vomiting 12 to 72 hours after infection.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Most people infected with Salmonella develop diarrhea, fever, abdominal cramps, and vomiting 12 to 72 hours after infection.","entailment":"The quote directly states the symptoms and the 12‑to‑72‑hour onset after infection.","population":"people infected with Salmonella","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"diarrhea, fever, abdominal cramps, vomiting","timeframe":"12 to 72 hours after infection","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Symptoms of Salmonella infection usually last 4 to 7 days and most people get better without treatment.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Symptoms usually last 4 to 7 days and most people get better without treatment.","entailment":"The quote indicates the typical duration of symptoms and that most recover without treatment.","population":"people with Salmonella infection","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"symptom duration 4 to 7 days, recovery without treatment","timeframe":"4 to 7 days","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Children, older adults, pregnant women, and people with weakened immune systems are at greater risk for severe Salmonella illness.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Certain people are at greater risk for severe illness and include children, older adults, pregnant women, and people with weakened immune systems (such as transplant patients and individuals with HIV/AIDS, cancer, and diabetes).","entailment":"The quote lists specific groups that have higher risk for severe illness.","population":"children, older adults, pregnant women, immunocompromised individuals","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"greater risk for severe illness","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"FDA requires all cartons of shell eggs that have not been treated to destroy Salmonella to carry a safe handling statement.","claimType":"methodological","quote":"FDA requires all cartons of shell eggs that have not been treated to destroy Salmonella to carry this safe handling statement:","entailment":"The quote states the regulatory requirement for labeling of untreated shell eggs.","population":"all cartons of shell eggs not treated to destroy Salmonella","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"must carry safe handling statement","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"To prevent illness from bacteria, eggs should be kept refrigerated, cooked until yolks are firm, and foods containing eggs should be cooked thoroughly.","claimType":"value","quote":"To prevent illness from bacteria: keep eggs refrigerated, cook eggs until yolks are firm, and cook foods containing eggs thoroughly.","entailment":"The quote gives recommended actions to prevent bacterial illness.","population":"","intervention":"keep eggs refrigerated; cook eggs until yolks are firm; cook foods containing eggs thoroughly","comparator":"","outcome":"prevention of illness","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"Eggs should be bought only if sold from a refrigerator or refrigerated case.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Buy eggs only if sold from a refrigerator or refrigerated case.","entailment":"The quote advises that purchase should be limited to eggs displayed in refrigerated conditions.","population":"eggs for purchase","intervention":"buy eggs only if sold from a refrigerator or refrigerated case","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"Eggs should be stored in a clean refrigerator at a temperature of 40°F or below.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Store promptly in a clean refrigerator at a temperature of 40° F or below. Use a refrigerator thermometer to check.","entailment":"The quote specifies the storage condition of a clean refrigerator at ≤40°F.","population":"eggs","intervention":"store in clean refrigerator at ≤40°F","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"Hard‑cooked eggs should be used or eaten within one week after cooking.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Use or eat hard‑cooked eggs (in the shell or peeled) within 1 week after cooking.","entailment":"The quote sets a one‑week limit for using or eating hard‑cooked eggs after cooking.","population":"hard‑cooked eggs","intervention":"use or eat within 1 week after cooking","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"within 1 week after cooking","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"Egg dishes containing eggs should be cooked to an internal temperature of 160°F.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Casseroles and other dishes containing eggs should be cooked to 160° F. Use a food thermometer to be sure.","entailment":"The quote directs that egg‑containing dishes be cooked to 160°F.","population":"egg‑containing dishes","intervention":"cook to 160°F","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"Cooked eggs or egg dishes should not be left out of the refrigerator for more than 2 hours, or more than 1 hour when temperatures exceed 90°F.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Never leave cooked eggs or egg dishes out of the refrigerator for more than 2 hours or for more than 1 hour when temperatures are above 90° F.","entailment":"The quote limits the time cooked eggs can be unrefrigerated based on ambient temperature.","population":"cooked eggs or egg dishes","intervention":"leave out of refrigerator","comparator":"more than 2 hours (or >1 hour above 90°F)","outcome":"risk of bacterial growth","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"normative"}]}
